observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to issue a justification letter regarding the deficiency in budget appropriation for loan payment. Additionally, the MBO should review the related loan amortization schedule to confirm the actual monthly amortization amount, which should serve as the basis for budget appropriation; and
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to obtain a copy of the credit memo from the bank and submit it to the accounting office for recording.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to immediately record the settlement of the CAs made under check nos. 381542 and 107837 respectively based on the TLs duly receipted by ICFAU, in compliance with Section 6.2.4.4 Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Require the MENR Officer to obtain copy of supporting documents from the donor and forward them to the Accounting Office for proper recording.  Additionally, instruct him to locate the solar-powered 4G Camera Kit and report its location to the GSO.; and
Require the GSO-Designate to count all properties and equipment of LGU whether donated or procured.
Furthermore, we recommended that all official and employees of the LGU report all donations received on behalf of the LGU as well as their supporting documents to the accounting office to facilitate recording in conformance to the above-mentioned guidelines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Acting Municipal Treasurer (AMT):
To strengthen the internal control policy over the collection and remittance of community tax fees of barangay treasurers to avoid the incurrence of late remittances and possible misuse or risk of loss of government funds;
To issue a memorandum or office order to officially designate the selected barangay treasurers as deputized collectors; and
To strictly adhere to the guidelines stipulated under Section 29, Chapter 3 of MNGAS.

We recommended that:
The Local Chief Executive, the planning staff, and the administrators of the facilities to utilize the completed projects for their intended purpose.
The Management ensure that PPAs are judiciously planned and their implementation are regularly monitored to avoid non-utilization of completed infrastructure projects and to ensure effective and efficient use of funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to require the concerned contractor to immediately rectify the defects considering that it is within the defects-liability warranty period. Otherwise, Management shall undertake such repair works and shall be entitled to full reimbursement of expenses incurred from the contractor to prevent further damage and deterioration of the structures.

We recommended that the LCE direct:
All End-Users / Requisitioning Departments to carefully prepare their Purchase Request inclusive of technical specifications and should be in accordance with their needs; and
The BAC to include the technical specification in the procurement documents of the items requested by the end-user.

We recommended that LCE direct all requisitioning departments to only include the technical specifications of items requested and exclude the brand names in the PRs prepared.
Prospectively, the Management shall ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184 particularly under Section 18 on the prohibition of brand names.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the BAC to submit to this office the copy of Certificate of PHILGEPS registration of the subject suppliers showing their registration number as well as the copy of the Mayor’s Permit/ Business Permit and Omnibus Sworn Statement.
Prospectively, the LCE shall direct the BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 8.5.1 and 54.6 and Annex H (Appendix A) of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184 on ensuring the submission of PHILGEPS registration number and other documentary requirements before the award of contracts undertaken through alternative modes of procurement.

We recommended that the LCE inform the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity about any postings of project signboards and/or public notices for validation by the AT, as required in COA Circular 2013-004.
Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer ensure that the tarpaulin posted at the location of the infrastructure projects conforms to the format stipulated in Annex A of COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure compliance on documentary requirements in all stages of the procurement process; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to defer processing of claims for payment of infrastructure projects until the required supporting documents are attached to the claims.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to submit a written justification for the inconsistencies noted on the computation of the OSH component in the ABC with the provisions of DPWH DO No. 197 series of 2016 dated October 7, 2016;
Require the contractor to refund the excess amount paid or deduct from remaining claim/s as necessary, should the justification be found by the Audit Team as untenable; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure the reasonableness of the computation of the ABC for all infrastructure projects by adhering to the provisions of applicable laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the five (5) DVs and its supporting documents otherwise, these will be suspended in audit.

We recommended that management require the concerned officials to refund the excess cost of transportation totalling ₱37,100.00 as computed above.
Also, we recommended that all officials and employees of the Municipality strictly adhere to the rules and regulations and rates of expenses and allowances for official travel prescribed in E.O. No. 77.

We recommended that the Management directs the GSO-designate to issue an Inventory Custodian Slip for all semi-expendable properties and equipment provided to officials and employees of the Municipality. Furthermore, the Management should strictly adhere to the accountability guidelines outlined in Section 4.1 of COA Circular 2022-004, dated May 31, 2022, for future issuances to end-users.
We recommended that the Management instruct the Focal Person to submit GAD Reports within the period prescribed by Section 5 of COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to reconcile the variance between the books and the RPCPPE totaling ₱3,541,841.26 of the Flood Control System, Other Infrastructure Assets and Other Machineries & Equipment accounts and to adjust or correct the records maintained by each department. We also recommend strict compliance with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and GSO to maintain the PPELC and PC respectively in compliance with the above-cited COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and the Municipal Accountant are still working on maintaining and updating the PPELC and PC for all PPEs acquired by the LGU
observations.reason: The GSO has yet to maintain a property card while the Municipal Accountant already has a PPELC for recently acquired PPEs but has not yet included PPEs acquired in previous years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant (MA):

1. To make necessary adjusting entries in the books for those stale checks as of December 31, 2022;

2. To set up payables/surplus if checks were not presented by the payee to the bank after six months from the date of its issuance; and

3. To record surplus if the stale checks were more than two years from the date of its issuance.

Also, we recommended that the LCE require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check at least one month before a check becomes stale.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant:

1. To verify if accounts payable outstanding for over two years has no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not covered by perfected contracts on record; and

2. To ensure that claims are substantiated with complete documentation, otherwise revert said balances to related account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the: 

1.  Disbursing Officer (DO) to liquidate his cash advances in the total amount of ₱6,430,363.36 and prospectively, adhere with Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002, dated February 10, 1997 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445.  

2. Municipal Accountant not to grant/approve disbursement vouchers for cash advances if the previous one was not yet liquidated.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to issue certification of no unliquidated cash advance prior to her approval of the DV to the official who made cash advance provided without unsettled previous CA, in compliance with Section 1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular NO. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials who were in charge of the distribution of relief goods amounting ₱1.74 million to provide proof of distribution such as RDS to be used by the Audit Team in validation, in compliance with Item VI of COA Circular No. 2014-009.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

1.  Require the coordination between MA and GSO towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording supplies and materials to ensure accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials;

2. Require the GSO to prepare the SSMIs weekly supported with RIS for supplies and materials issued and the MA to prepare journal entry voucher supported by SSMIs and RIS submitted by the GSO to record issuance of supplies and materials; and

3. Instruct the MA and GSO to maintain SLC and stock cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct/withheld retention money or require special bank guarantee before processing the payment. Adhere strictly with the provision prescribed in Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR-RA 9184.

We reiterate our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer (AMT) to submit the CRAAF accompanied by the RAAF of all accountable officers to the Audit Team, through the Accounting Office not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month as provided under the NGAS manual.

We commended Management for the substantial implementation of the PPAs under the 20% DF through their systematic planning and strategic execution which achieved desired benefits to the community within the set timeline.  Nevertheless, we recommend that the Municipal Development Council and all other concerned offices of the Municipality implement fully all PPAs targeted during the year within the timeframe to ensure that anticipated local developments will be realized.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

a. the MHO to render a report to the Municipal Accountant on the receipt, issuance/utilization, and balances of vaccines directly received from outside sources; and 

b. the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MHO, to determine the full cost of ending inventory of vaccines as at year-end, if any and henceforth recognize and record future receipts and issuances of the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:

1. The AMT and Municipal Assessor to re-visit the information of real properties uploaded in the ETRACS to verify the accuracy of data, especially those pertaining to abnormal balances and to consult with ETRACS technical support, if needed; and

2. The MA in coordination with the MTO, to establish the correct amount of RPT and SET receivable at the beginning of the year.

2. The BAC to ensure the bidders’ capacity to provide and deliver services or goods before declaring the winning bidder.

Compel the concerned accountable officers to liquidate immediately their respective cash advances, otherwise cause the immediate withholding of salary of the erring officers pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) requires the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to reconcile the discrepancies between the inventory report and accounting records.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the:
Municipal Engineer and/or the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to properly plan and complete the detailed engineering works before proceeding to the procurement of civil works to avoid major changes in the design and specifications of the project;
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages against the contractor for incurring delay in the completion of the Municipal Building; and
Impose other sanction such as termination of contract, forfeiture of performance security and black listing of the contractor if so warranted.

We recommended that the LCE:
Institute immediately the termination of consultancy contract;
Cause the forfeiture of Performance Security, and
Impose other applicable penalties such as liquidated damages including the blacklisting of the consulting firm, if  warranted

We recommended that the accounting personnel be diligent in discharging their functions and duties especially in recording of transactions to avoid misrepresentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended that immediate adjustments be made in the books of accounts to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant present the Loans Payable-Domestic amounting to ₱54.53 million as of December 31, 2018 with non-current liability portion of ₱50.47 million and current liability portion of ₱4.06 million in the Statement of Financial Position.  Also, disclose in the financial statement the complete details of the loans payable including amount due to be settled after twelve months from the reporting date to present fairly the financial statement of the agency and in accordance with paragraph 70, 71 and 80  of PPSAS Volume 1.

We recommended that Management require the MHRM Officer together with concerned Department Heads or create a committee that would formulate standard policy guidelines to strengthen internal controls on hiring/rehiring of JOs with the end in view of:
limiting the number of hired/rehired personnel based on actual manpower need by each office; and
assuring that JOs do not perform functions of existing regular employees and declaring that there will be no duplication of works among JOs assigned in one Office.
As preparatory procedure, we suggested that the agency review existing functions, systems and procedures, organizational structure and staffing to determine the appropriate manpower complement for their programs/activities/projects in accordance with Section 90 of the General Provisions of the FY 2019 General Appropriations Act (GAA).

We recommended that the LCE direct the offices concerned in the implementation of programs/projects to submit on time copies of MOA or any other contracts relative to fund transfers from NGAs so that utilization of funds and the proper disposition of unexpended excess fund transfers after the completion of projects could be evaluated properly.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and project proponents to evaluate the need for the unexpended balances of the fund from the national government agencies and if found no longer needed, return the same  to the source agency in order that the funds can be utilized for other necessary projects of the government.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to use the prescribed forms and contents of the bidding documents to encourage participation of more prospective bidders to attain the most advantageous price quotation.  Also, we enjoined the BAC to review and ensure that the timelines of procurement activities contained in REI and IB are exactly the same to avoid confusion among prospective bidders.

We recommended that the LCE construct a garage to secure the vehicles and equipment from burglars and prevent them from direct exposure to natural elements such as rain and sunlight that hasten their deterioration as provided under paragraph 5 COA Circular No. 75-6 and Section 2 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipality seek immediately the Authority to Close Controlled Dumpsite from DENR-EMB Region V.  Such action is necessary to address unauthorized closure and implementation of the Closure Plan.
We also recommended that Management furnish immediately the Auditor with the approved closure plan as soon as the ATC is issued for our reference in audit.

We recommended that the LCE require the:
Municipal General Services Officer to initiate the procurement/bidding of all projects chargeable to the 20% DF in the first quarter of each year to prevent delay in procurement process;
Municipal Planning and Development Officer to give equal priority to the implementation of projects funded from different funds, including from 20% DF.  This is to promote/encourage an optimum and efficient utilization of all funds and achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and
Municipal Planning and Development Officer to plan activities properly and efficiently to ensure that all targeted projects and activities are implemented as scheduled so that intended beneficiaries can enjoy the benefits that could be derived from the projects once completed.

We recommended that the LDRRMC monitor and evaluate regularly the status of implementation of the LDRRMP in compliance with Section 11 of RA 10121. Ensure that PPAs that need to be undertaken in advance are given priority in the implementation of projects to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters.
Specifically, we recommended the following measures:
the MDRRM Officer to prepare and require the office concerned to  submit to the General Services Office all Purchase Requests for all projects containing details of supplies, materials and equipment to be procured chargeable to the 5% LDRRMF in the first quarter of each year to prevent delay on procurement process.  In turn, the GSO,  Budget Office, Mayor’s Office and Bids and Awards Committee facilitate the procurement process; and
the MDRRM Officer to plan properly all programs/projects/activities funded from 70% Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events or conditions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and
Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection including enforcing levy on real property as imposed by Sections 256 and 258 of RA 7160.

We recommended the following courses of action for the Management:

To direct the inventory committee to conduct physical inventory of all its PPE and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment with complete data to COA for verification.

To require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to reconcile their records and adjust any difference noted to come up with accurate balances of PPEs presented in the financial statements as of December 31, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted partial inventory report of PPEs.
observations.reason: GSO is still on reconciliation of records with the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) take appropriate and immediate action to replenish in full the cash requirement or cash back-up for the total liability accounts.  Likewise we recommended that the LCE assure strict compliance with Section 305(e) and 309(b) of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 94 Volume I of NGAS Manual, Volume I for LGUs which provides that trust fund shall only be used for specific purpose for which it was created or for which it comes into the possession of the local government unit.

We recommended the following: 
Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001 on the allocation of specific amount for POP in the annual budget of the LGU from where the amount of 30% allocation for Confidential Fund shall be based. 

Submit a 3-year Peace and Order and Public Safety Plan, and Resolution duly approved by two-thirds (2/3) of the total membership of the Local Peace and Order Council authorizing the release and utilization of the total Confidential Fund. 

Submit written explanation or reason why specific appropriation for POP was not included in the 2017 approved annual budget of the LGU
